analogClaims.claim: real-time live preview of new names before applying, with changed parts highlighted
full undo / operation history reverts the whole batch (e.g. 100-operation undo, Cmd-Z)
conflict detection when two files would map to the same name
regex find & replace with capture groups plus multiple rule types
drag-and-drop files and folders to load them
EXIF/audio metadata-based renaming for power users

baselineRequirements: load files via drag-and-drop or a folder picker
live preview with highlighted changes before anything is applied
regex find & replace with capture groups, plus common rules (numbering, case, replace)
full undo of an applied batch
customDelta: emphasis on speed and simplicity over power-user feature sprawl
successCriteria: user previews every new name before any file is touched
user can fully undo an applied batch
no file is overwritten because of a name conflict
acceptanceTests: loading 10 files and typing a find/replace shows live-updated names with changes highlighted, and no file is modified yet
applying a batch then choosing undo restores every original name
a rule mapping two files to the same name is flagged and blocked before apply
dataFlows: reads file names and metadata from user-selected folders; writes (renames) only on explicit apply; nothing leaves the machine
permissions: file-system access to user-selected folders (core function — confirm scope)
unknowns: the user did not specify a unique twist — scope defaults to a market-standard renamer
should it support metadata (EXIF) renaming, or stay simple?
mustAskIfDiscovered: any network call or cloud upload
deleting files rather than renaming them

decisionLog.decision: default to a simple rule set (replace, numbering, case) over a power-user sprawl
decisionLog.rationale: UI/UX detail aligned with the request for simplicity; decided for the user

decisionLog.decision: require an explicit Apply; never rename on keystroke
decisionLog.rationale: safety — a destructive file operation must be gated behind preview + confirm

decisionLog.decision: file-system access scoped to user-selected folders
decisionLog.rationale: core function but touches the user's files — confirm the scope, least privilege

unresolvedRisks: renaming is a destructive file operation — undo and conflict detection are mandatory, not optional
file-system access must be limited to user-selected folders only (least privilege)
